summary: Ukrainian intelligence services destroyed Russian supply vessels and a S‑400 radar station in occupied Crimea. Russian forces launched a salvo of Iskander‑M missiles and 189 Shahed drones across Ukraine, with air defenses downing most. Intense drone activity continues on multiple fronts, targeting fuel stations, infrastructure and civilian areas.
key_points: Ukrainian SBU sank Russian supply ships "Volga" and "Vyatka" and the ferry "Petropavlovsk" in occupied Crimea and struck a S‑400 radar station covering the Kerch Strait (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Russia fired seven Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 189 Shahed drones nationwide, with air defenses intercepting three missiles and 174 drones (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
A Shahed drone hit a school bus in Nikopol, killing two people and wounding twelve, including two 12‑year‑old girls (Source: @UkraineNow/82209).
Multiple targeted attacks on fuel stations were recorded in Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions, resulting in civilian casualties and vehicle fires (Source: @eRadarrua/98259).
Ukrainian FPV drones struck a power plant in Kremenchuk and a chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, causing infrastructure damage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68068; @UkraineNow/82184).
Russian MiG‑31K fighter launched with an X‑47M2 Kinzhal aerobalistic missile, prompting a nationwide alert (Source: @eRadarrua/98283).
Russia awarded former Belgorod region head Gladkov the Order of Alexander Nevsky (Source: @tass_agency/382539).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces eliminated Russian supply vessels and a S‑400 radar site in occupied Crimea, marking a significant intelligence‑driven strike. Russia responded with a broad Iskander‑M and Shahed drone barrage, though most were intercepted by Ukrainian air defenses. Continued high‑tempo drone attacks on fuel stations, power infrastructure and civilian targets intensify the conflict across multiple fronts.

points: Ukrainian SBU sank the Russian supply ships "Volga" and "Vyatka" and the ferry "Petropavlovsk" in occupied Crimea, and hit a S‑400 radar station covering the Kerch Strait (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Ukrainian FPV drones struck the Kremenchuk hydro‑electric plant, prompting local air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68068).
Ukrainian forces used drones to attack a Russian chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, damaging power lines and a residential house (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
Ukrainian intelligence reported a drone strike on the design engineering department of the Zaporizhzhia NPP, causing a fire and civilian injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178).
Ukrainian units reported a Shahed drone hitting a school bus in Nikopol, killing two and wounding twelve, including two girls (Source: @UkraineNow/82209).
Russia launched seven Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 189 Shahed drones across Ukraine; air defenses downed three missiles and 174 drones (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Russia awarded former Belgorod region head Gladkov the Order of Alexander Nevsky (Source: @tass_agency/382539).
A Russian MiG‑31K fighter carried an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile, prompting a nationwide alert (Source: @eRadarrua/98283).
Russian forces continued targeted strikes on fuel stations in Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk, causing civilian casualties and vehicle fires (Source: @eRadarrua/98259).
Russian artillery and drone attacks damaged industrial facilities in Kremenchuk, causing a partial blackout (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Ukrainian drones repeatedly struck fuel stations in Zaporizhzhia region, including a WOG station in Osypenky, injuring civilians (Source: @eRadarrua/98259).
Shahed drones hit Nikopol and surrounding settlements, damaging an A‑Z station and causing civilian injuries (Source: @eRadarrua/98259).
Ukrainian UAVs targeted the design engineering department of the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, igniting a fire (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178).
Russian artillery and drone attacks on Kremenchuk caused a partial blackout and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed drone strike on a school bus in Nikopol, resulting in two deaths and twelve injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82209).
A UAV was observed over northern Kharkiv heading toward Kharkiv city and Bohodukhiv (Source: @kpszsu/66476).
Reactive UAVs from the Black Sea region were reported heading toward Odesa and Zatoka (Source: @kpszsu/66508).
UAV activity was noted near the border of Chernihiv region around Slavutych (Source: @eRadarrua/98270).
A UAV was reported on the Kharkiv‑Zachepylivka direction in western Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/66480).
Ukrainian intelligence posted a UAV near the Kyiv‑Zhytomyr border in the Krasatychi area (Source: @eRadarrua/98276).
Ukrainian drones struck a private household and a food‑delivery vehicle in the Kherson region, injuring civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178).
A Shahed drone attack on a civilian fuel station in Nikopol killed a 64‑year‑old man (Source: @eRadarrua/98259).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a residential house in the Kherson district of Kalanchak, wounding a civilian (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178).
Russian attacks on the Odesa region damaged energy and civil infrastructure, leaving parts of the community without power (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Ukrainian UAVs targeted a chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, causing damage to power lines and a residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted three Iskander‑M missiles and 174 out of 189 Shahed drones launched by Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
A Russian MiG‑31K fighter launched an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile, triggering a nationwide alert (Source: @eRadarrua/98283).
Ukrainian command claimed Russia plans to increase FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year‑end (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193).
Ukrainian forces reported downing 660 enemy drones in a single night over various fronts (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
Ukrainian radar detected a single Shahed drone heading toward the Kremenchuk hydro‑electric plant (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68072).

key_developments: Ukrainian SBU sank Russian supply vessels "Volga" and "Vyatka" and hit a S‑400 radar station in occupied Crimea, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Russia launched a large‑scale Iskander‑M and Shahed drone barrage, with most weapons intercepted by Ukrainian air defenses (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Multiple fuel station attacks in Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions caused civilian casualties and vehicle fires, indicating a focus on logistics disruption (Source: @eRadarrua/98259).
A Shahed drone strike on a school bus in Nikopol killed two and wounded twelve, highlighting civilian vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/82209).
Ukrainian drones struck the Kremenchuk hydro‑electric plant and a Russian chemical plant in Novomoskovsk, affecting critical infrastructure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68068; @UkraineNow/82184).
Russia deployed a MiG‑31K with an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile, prompting a nationwide alert and demonstrating advanced missile capability (Source: @eRadarrua/98283).
Russian authorities awarded former Belgorod region head Gladkov the Order of Alexander Nevsky, reflecting internal political developments (Source: @tass_agency/382539).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying long‑range missile and drone strikes, targeting logistics hubs, energy infrastructure and civilian centers across Ukraine. Ukrainian counter‑measures have proven effective in intercepting the majority of aerial threats, yet repeated fuel‑station attacks indicate a sustained effort to disrupt supply lines. The deployment of advanced air‑launched missiles such as the Kinzhal raises the risk of high‑impact strikes on strategic assets, while the increased use of FPV drones on the front lines suggests a shift toward low‑cost, high‑frequency attacks that strain Ukrainian air‑defence resources.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine has mobilised air‑defence units nationwide, achieving high interception rates for missiles and drones, and continues to conduct targeted strikes on Russian logistical assets in occupied Crimea. International partners are monitoring the escalation, with NATO members likely to assess additional air‑defence support. Russian political gestures, such as awarding senior officials, aim to bolster domestic morale amid the operational pressure.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian intelligence reports indicate a coordinated campaign against Russian supply vessels and radar installations in Crimea, suggesting enhanced maritime surveillance capabilities. The concentration of drone attacks on fuel stations across multiple oblasts points to a systematic effort to impair Russian fuel logistics. Continued sightings of UAVs on the northern and eastern fronts underscore the need for persistent air‑defence readiness and rapid response to low‑altitude threats.
